hero.subTagline: Real-time social infrastructure on an object-oriented financial chain.

chain.title: Why a New Chain
chain.intro: Pado runs on Nasun, a high-performance blockchain forked from Sui. The choice to build a dedicated L1 is architectural, not ideological.

features.title: Object-Based State Model
features.description: Every asset, position, and market exists as an independent on-chain object with its own ownership and access rules. Objects can be composed, transferred, and modified without touching global state.

features.title: Parallel Execution
features.description: Transactions that touch independent objects execute simultaneously. A spot trade and a lending deposit process in the same block without contention, enabling throughput that scales with object diversity.

features.title: DAG-Based Consensus
features.description: Narwhal-Bullshark consensus provides deterministic ordering with minimal latency. The DAG structure significantly reduces the surface area for MEV extraction and front-running.

features.title: Programmable Transactions
features.description: Multiple market actions compose into a single atomic transaction. Supply collateral, open a derivatives position, and set a stop-loss — all in one block, all or nothing.

social.title: Social Infrastructure
social.intro: The social layer is not an add-on. It is core infrastructure built with the same rigor as the trading engine — real-time, authenticated, and integrated into every surface.

chat.title: Chat Server
chat.description: WebSocket-based real-time messaging with cryptographic signature authentication. Every message is tied to a verified on-chain identity. SQLite persistence ensures message history survives reconnections. Rate limiting and DoS prevention protect the service at scale.

rooms.title: Room Architecture
rooms.description: Multi-room system supporting market-specific channels, general discussion, and structured conversations. Reply threading enables focused discussion within active rooms. Nicknames provide readable identity without sacrificing wallet-level verification.

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard Engine
leaderboard.description: Rankings computed from on-chain trading data processed by the Rust event indexer. No self-reported metrics, no off-chain manipulation. Every position, every PnL, every rank is verifiable against the chain.

auth.title: Auth Bridge
auth.description: Trading and social use the same keypair. Sign a trade and sign a message with the same identity. zkLogin users authenticate to the chat server with the same ephemeral key used for transactions — no separate login, no additional friction.

account.title: Smart Account Architecture
account.intro: Users interact through a non-custodial smart account that replaces seed phrases with modern authentication while preserving full key control.

features.title: zkLogin
features.description: Users authenticate via Google OAuth. An ephemeral keypair is generated locally, linked to the OAuth identity through a zero-knowledge proof, and used to sign transactions. The OAuth provider never sees the private key. The ZK proof verifies identity without revealing it.

features.title: Multi-Signer & Social Recovery
features.description: Accounts support multiple signers with configurable thresholds. Guardian-based social recovery allows account restoration without seed phrases or centralized intermediaries.

features.title: Session Permissions
features.description: For high-frequency trading, users can delegate constrained authority to session keys. These keys have limited scope (specific markets, max amounts) and time-bound validity, reducing authentication friction without compromising security.

features.title: Key Encryption
features.description: Private keys are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations). Keys are cleared from memory after use. Rate limiting prevents brute-force attacks with progressive lockout thresholds.

markets.title: Market Infrastructure
markets.intro: All markets execute natively on-chain through Move smart contracts. No off-chain matching, no centralized sequencer.

deepbook.title: DeepBook V3 — Central Limit Order Book
deepbook.description: Fully on-chain CLOB with price-time priority matching. Supports limit orders, market orders, and fill-or-kill semantics. A Rust-based indexer processes DeepBook events in real-time for the trading UI.

prediction.title: Prediction Markets
prediction.description: Binary YES/NO outcome markets with dedicated orderbooks per outcome. NUSDC-collateralized. Markets resolve on-chain via admin oracle or automated resolution.

lottery.title: On-Chain Lottery
lottery.description: Weekly draws using Sui Random (VRF) for provable fairness. Users pick 5 numbers from 1-32 (201,376 combinations). Three-tier prize distribution: 60% jackpot, 25% second, 15% third.

oracle.title: DevOracle — Price Feeds
oracle.description: Admin-controlled price oracle providing BTC, ETH, and NASUN feeds. Feeds update every 30 seconds via CoinGecko/Binance polling. Powers margin calculations, liquidations, and perpetual funding rates.

margin.title: Capital Infrastructure
margin.intro: A portfolio-level risk engine evaluates all positions under a single margin framework. Risk is computed per account, not per protocol. This is the infrastructure that keeps capital flowing — and keeps users from needing to leave.

collateral.title: Multi-Collateral System
items: Collateral objects transition between states: idle, margin, lending

liquidation.title: Liquidation Mechanics
items: Partial liquidation: max 50% of position per event

cta.title: Dive Deeper
cta.body: Explore the architecture in detail or try Pado on devnet.
